title: Transparent Scheduled Maintenance Policy
purpose: Let the site keep itself useful through visible GitHub workflows, logs, reports, generated pages, public artifacts and clear evidence boundaries.
mission: Expose wrongdoing, help humanity, classify evidence, connect trackers, and map concentrated power through public records and clearly labelled speculation.
visibilityRule: Every automated maintenance run must be visible through a workflow log, generated report, artifact, commit message, issue, or public change log.
allowedMaintenance: Generate public reports from tracker data already in the repository.
Refresh dashboards from public JSON sources.
Rebuild pages from existing data files.
Run content scrubbers that remove author-facing notes, placeholders and chat markers.
Run link audits, route audits, freshness checks and duplicate checks.
Update timer notes when linked tracker lanes change evidence level.
Create or update downloadable JSON and Markdown briefs.
Commit generated reports with a clear message when outputs change.
blockedMaintenance: Publishing private victim identities or illegal material.
Making direct accusations against private people without source-grade evidence.
Removing logs or reports to hide mistakes.
speculationRule: Speculation is allowed when clearly labelled as speculation, hypothesis, motif, claim-history, open question or debunk lane. It must not be framed as proven fact unless source-grade records support it.
changeLogRule: Each maintenance run should produce a report showing what it checked, what changed, what failed, and what should be improved next.
upgradePriorityOrder: Fix broken deploys and broken routes.
Remove author-facing or machine-facing text from public pages.
Connect isolated pages into the tracker graph.
Add source links and downloadable briefs.
Expand speculation pages with labels and safe wording.
